prompt: A teammate asks: explain how a data room member's access to documents is enforced across backend APIs, group permissions, data room settings, and frontend visibility. Do not modify code. Produce an impact analysis and verification plan.

expected_files.reason: Defines data room, membership, permission, and related persistence models.
Core data room API entry; required to understand backend access checks.
Group permission API; central to member permission changes.
Frontend group permission UI; required to connect backend permission state to user actions.
Data room permission settings UI; required for configuration-level access behavior.
Group membership API; relevant to how member access is assigned.
Permission group API; relevant for group-level access management.
Frontend group member list; useful for visibility and permission verification.
Dataroom view path; useful for understanding viewer-side enforcement and analytics.

must_notice: Backend access checks and frontend visibility are separate; frontend visibility is not sufficient enforcement.
The model should distinguish team membership, data room membership, group permissions, and document-level access.
Verification should include unauthorized member and cross-team negative cases.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim a single frontend component enforces access by itself.
Do not invent role names or permission enums without reading schema/code.
Do not skip backend API checks.
scoring_notes: Use this task to test whether the model can follow auth beyond UI components.
